Project: Elatilo
Purpose: Expense registration system with budgets, receipts, approvals, attachments, authentication, and PostgreSQL-backed business rules.

Current stack:
- Backend: FastAPI + psycopg + PostgreSQL
- Frontend: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript
- UI extras: react-icons + xlsx + local brand assets
- Auth: JWT with python-jose, passlib, argon2
- Database: PostgreSQL with SQL functions, triggers, and startup compatibility scripts
- Deployment: Railway with separate backend/frontend services and GitHub Actions workflows

Repository structure:
- backend/
- frontend/
- DATABASE/
- project_brain/
- ARQUITECTURA/

Important backend architecture:
- backend/main.py creates FastAPI app
- backend/main.py contains _run_startup_migrations()
- backend/app/api/routes/ contains HTTP endpoints
- backend/app/services/ contains business orchestration
- backend/app/repositories/ contains SQL access
- backend/app/schemas/ contains Pydantic schemas
- backend/app/db/connection.py contains DB connection utilities

Startup database compatibility:
- FastAPI startup runs _run_startup_migrations()
- DB.SQL is executed on every startup
- DATABASE/migrations/*.sql are executed once
- applied migrations are tracked in app_migration_log

Current main entities:
- usuario
- t_proyectos
- presupuesto
- comprobante
- adjunto
- movimiento_presupuesto

Important database rules:
- proyecto is the parent context for presupuesto
- proyecto.nombre_proyecto must be unique
- proyecto can be archived/reactivated through activo flag
- presupuesto has its own currency
- comprobante has its own currency
- if receipt currency differs from budget currency, tipo_cambio is required
- monto_presupuesto stores converted amount in budget currency
- normal approval consumes budget saldo_disponible using monto_presupuesto
- CAJA_CHICA approval increases presupuesto.monto_total and saldo_disponible
- approval/rejection should go through SQL functions, not direct status updates

Important SQL functions:
- aprobar_comprobante()
- rechazar_comprobante()
- set_updated_at()
- blockers for editing/deleting approved receipts and their attachments

Auth endpoints:
- POST /auth/register
- POST /auth/login
- GET /auth/me

User endpoints:
- GET /usuarios
- GET /usuarios/{usuario_id}
- PATCH /usuarios/{usuario_id}
- PATCH /usuarios/{usuario_id}/activar
- PATCH /usuarios/{usuario_id}/desactivar

Budget endpoints:
- GET /presupuestos
- POST /presupuestos
- GET /presupuestos/{presupuesto_id}
- PATCH /presupuestos/{presupuesto_id}
- DELETE /presupuestos/{presupuesto_id}
- GET /presupuestos/{presupuesto_id}/movimientos

Receipt endpoints:
- GET /comprobantes
- POST /comprobantes
- GET /comprobantes/{comprobante_id}
- PATCH /comprobantes/{comprobante_id}
- DELETE /comprobantes/{comprobante_id}
- POST /comprobantes/{comprobante_id}/aprobar
- POST /comprobantes/{comprobante_id}/rechazar

Attachment endpoints:
- GET /comprobantes/{comprobante_id}/adjuntos
- POST /comprobantes/{comprobante_id}/adjuntos
- DELETE /comprobantes/{comprobante_id}/adjuntos/{adjunto_id}

Exchange-rate endpoints:
- GET /tipo-cambio/ventanilla

Log endpoints:
- GET /log
- POST /log
- PATCH /log/{log_id}

Frontend behavior:
- Login screen is decoupled from dashboard
- Dashboard only appears after authentication
- Frontend stores JWT in localStorage
- Frontend talks to backend through VITE_API_URL
- Vite resolves VITE_API_URL at build time
- frontend Dockerfile must expose VITE_API_URL during build with ARG/ENV
- App.tsx is still the main orchestrator, but projects/budgets/users/icons already have extracted modules
- First authenticated screen focuses on budgets
- The first authenticated budget entry is referred to visually as dashboard1
- dashboard1 now focuses on project maintenance and project listing
- each listed project has its own Presupuestos button with PiPiggyBankBold
- selected project opens a dedicated per-project budget section
- clicking a budget opens a dedicated budget dashboard
- Budget detail flow contains receipts, movements, and attachments scoped to that budget
- Frontend supports:
  - login/register
  - list/create/update users
  - activate/deactivate users
  - create/list/archive/activate projects
  - create/list budgets by project
  - create/list receipts
  - approve/reject receipts
  - list movements for selected budget
  - list/create/delete attachments for selected receipt
  - request exchange rate when receipt currency differs from budget currency
  - handle CAJA_CHICA in forms and lists
  - export approved receipts to Excel
  - switch between dark/light theme
  - browse a React icon gallery
  - display BCCR exchange rates dashboard
  - prioritize featured exchange-rate entities visually
  - create log entries and update them to PENDIENTE/COMPLETADO/RECHAZADO with comment
  - contextual per-action feedback below action buttons
  - budget detail summary card aligned structurally with receipt panels

Security hardening already implemented:
- docs/openapi are disabled when ENV=prod
- /health/db returns 404 in production
- weak/default JWT secret is blocked in production
- rate limiting exists for /, /health, /auth/login, /auth/register
- security headers middleware is enabled
- public registration is hidden by default
- ADMIN can still create users through /auth/register
- user listing is ADMIN-only
- user detail is self-or-admin
- inactive users cannot continue using old tokens
- receipt and attachment access is restricted by owner/role
- APROBADOR cannot approve own receipt; ADMIN can approve own receipt
- non-privileged users only see their own log entries

Current deployment lessons:
- if frontend points to 127.0.0.1 in production, suspect missing VITE_API_URL at build time
- use browser DevTools Console and Network early to verify the real Request URL
- GitHub Actions deploy workflows now use detached Railway deploys to reduce failures from build-log streaming

Current important decisions:
- keep backend layered and simple
- keep critical state transitions in SQL
- support multi-currency with explicit user-provided exchange rate
- support CAJA_CHICA as a budget-funding receipt type
- cast enum values explicitly in SQL CASE expressions when needed
- grow frontend features pragmatically while extracting reusable dashboards
- use project as the main navigation context before budgets
- default project filtering to active projects
- prefer direct action placement inside project cards over detached navigation controls
- use `Dashboard` as the global analytics surface
- support project coordinates for geographic visualization
- show project history with balance, expenses, and total-balance series

Known operational requirement:
- DATABASE_URL user must have enough DB permissions for startup compatibility scripts

Known pending items:
- frontend/index.html and frontend/vite.config.ts still look like Vite defaults
- user password change is still pending a backend endpoint
- App.tsx still needs more extraction despite the new modules
- visual polish is still iterative in the budget-detail header area
- attachment-related PostgreSQL column still needs expansion to fix `value too long for type character varying(100)`
- dependency compatibility around passlib/bcrypt should be reviewed because logs showed `module 'bcrypt' has no attribute '__about__'`

Useful project memory files:
- project_brain/decision.md
- project_brain/umldiagrams.md
- DATABASE/notes.md
- DATABASE/DB.SQL
